# Convention: kt = ct_static * rho * D^4
# where D is in METERS
column3: and motor speed omega is in RAD/S.

column1: # Thrust = kt * omega^2
# These are CALIBRATED static coefficients in the rad/s convention
# aerospace-standard CT(n) with n in revolutions/sec. To convert to standard:
#   CT_aero = ct_static * (2*pi)^2 ~ 39.5x larger
# (e.g. 5x4.5x3 ct_static 2.745e-3 -> CT_aero 0.108
# BENCH CALIBRATION (2026-05-30): ct_static is now PHYSICAL — a real static
# thrust coefficient (CT_aero ~0.09-0.12 across all FPV prop sizes)
# legacy per-size-anchored formula values (which over-produced thrust ~4x and
# had a non-physical diameter dependence). Basis:
#   cq_static = ct_static * 0.348*(0.010+0.002*pitch+0.002*blades) / D_meters
# The absolute level is anchored to the iFlight XING2 2207 bench report
# (1576 g pull and 40.35 A at 16 V / full throttle
# the torque-balance loaded-RPM model it reproduces bench max-thrust within ~2%.
# Thrust scales with D^4 via kt (physics)
# Relative ratios across (pitch
#   - Per inch of pitch: ~6-8% more thrust at same RPM (0.7 + 0.06*pitch).
#   - Blade count: ~15% per-blade spread (2 vs 3 blade)
# ACCURACY NOTE: max-thrust is bench-anchored for 5"-class motors (the XING2
# 2207 anchor). The loaded-RPM factor is a single constant (0.88 of no-load);
# this is accurate for well-matched mid-KV motors but over-credits tiny high-KV
# motors (a 0802 12000KV bogs to ~0.5 of no-load)
column2: so micro builds read low on

# above
